result:
Design complete and pre-registered in `runs/pending_experiment.json` (figcheck PASS). The experiment: **convergence-scaled training — 3× optimizer steps with cosine LR decay to zero, under the kept fresh-draw sampler**. Only `model/train.py` changes; the deployed model is untouched.

**Why this, and not another auxiliary-loss variant:** the last three experiments (22, 23/24) were all reverted, so the plateau rule applies — and a fresh read-only probe I ran against the actual kept exp-20 model (`runs/_probe_iter5_evalcells.py`) shows the premise behind all three is stale. The ~3× train-vs-eval generalization gap they attacked is *gone*: berlin midday train-split crops now localize at 1014 m vs 1017 m for eval crops (pre-exp-20 probes measured 267–400 m on train). Exp 20's single-exposure resampling removed memorization, and with it the model stopped fitting anything precisely — it underfits uniformly, on both splits. Two more pieces of evidence: training loss is still falling ~0.065/epoch at the epoch-8 cutoff with a never-decayed constant LR, and the same probe rejected my own alternative suspicion (the decode does *not* structurally avoid the never-trained eval-block cells — 13–21% vs a 20% base rate). It also reinterprets the three reverts: their auxiliary losses competed for the same ~4,500 already-insufficient optimizer steps.

The change picks the one plateau-rule family never tried — training-scale (exp 7 scaled data, never optimization): 24 fresh-draw epochs instead of 8, with per-step cosine annealing so the run actually converges. Memory profile per epoch is byte-identical to the proven baseline (the exp-23 OOM lesson is restated in the brief); wall time roughly triples to ~1 hour of training.

**Pre-registered prediction:** 839.12 → ~630–760 m if optimization is binding. The falsification branch is explicitly informative: if the loss drops ≥0.25 nats but the metric moves <5%, optimization is exonerated and capacity/architecture becomes the demonstrated next bottleneck for the following iteration.

The implementation brief gives file-exact instructions (EPOCH_MULT constant, scheduler placement, untouched contracts: CLI flags and the 1×3×128×128 → [[u, v, conf]] ONNX export). The gallery figure reuses exp 20's validated inference-path drawing with red reserved for the one change: an LR-schedule inset showing the old constant-LR hard stop versus the new cosine glide over the 3×-longer run. The harness can take it from here.

tool_input.description: Run probe with repo on PYTHONPATH
Run eval-cell avoidance probe on kept model

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=20;" > /tmp/exp20_svg.txt && wc -c /tmp/exp20_svg.txt && sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT length(arch_svg), substr(arch_svg,1,120) FROM experiments WHERE id IN (20,22,24);"
which rsvg-convert inkscape convert 2>/dev/null; .venv/bin/pip list 2>/dev/null | grep -i -E "svg|cairo|playwright"
